title: Apply Now: 2026 Internships at the U.S. Trade and Development Agency (USTDA)
summary: Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in global development, infrastructure, and international partnerships? The U.S. Trade and Development Agency (USTDA) is offering a range of internship opportunities for motivated students and young professionals eager to contribute to impactful projects in emerging markets. USTDA plays a key role as the U.S. government s first mover on critical infrastructure development , helping to advance shared strategic priorities while fostering sustainable economic growth worldwide. Through its internship program, you can work alongside experienced professionals and gain exposure to international development, trade, and policy implementation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Available Internship Openings 1. Office of Programs Internship Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of Programs Overview: Interns in this office will support USTDA s core project development activities, contributing to initiatives that drive critical infrastructure development in emerging economies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); 2. Intern, Office of Administration / Human Resources Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of Administration / HR Overview: This role offers experience in organizational operations, talent management, and HR systems that support the agency s global mission and internal workforce development. 3. Intern, Office of the Chief Information Officer Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) Overview: Interns will assist with IT systems, digital infrastructure, and information management, supporting the technological backbone of USTDA s global operations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Why Apply? An internship with USTDA offers you the chance to: Gain real-world experience in international development and trade Work on projects that support infrastructure growth in emerging markets Develop professional skills in policy, administration, technology, and project management Build a global professional network within a U.S. government agency Contribute to meaningful initiatives that drive economic development and sustainability Application Deadline All internship applications close on March 20, 2026 . Early applications are strongly encouraged. To apply to any of the internship opportunities, Visit Here Take the Next Step If you are passionate about global development, infrastructure, and international collaboration, these internships at the U.S. Trade and Development Agency provide a valuable platform to launch your career. Prepare your application materials early, tailor your resume to the role you re applying for, and highlight your interest in international development and infrastructure. Start your journey toward a global career today. Discover more Internship opportunities Here. title: Harper Fellowship 2026–2027 at The 19th for Emerging Journalists
summary: The Harper Fellowship 2026 2027 at The 19th is now accepting applications from emerging journalists interested in developing their careers in reporting, audience engagement, news product development, and nonprofit media leadership. The Harper Fellowship application deadline is March 20, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. PST, and the program offers a year-long salaried fellowship with full benefits and extensive professional development opportunities. About the Harper Fellowship Program The Harper Fellowship at The 19th is a prestigious journalism fellowship named after Frances Ellen Watkins Harper, widely recognized as the mother of African-American journalism. The program was launched in 2022 to create meaningful opportunities for graduates and alumni connected to Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) to gain experience in nonprofit journalism. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through the Harper Fellowship program, selected fellows spend a full year working with The 19th s editorial, product, audience engagement, and development teams. Fellows are embedded in their respective departments and contribute to meaningful journalism projects while gaining hands-on newsroom experience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The Harper Fellowship focuses on strengthening diversity in journalism while providing fellows with mentorship, professional training, and career guidance that supports their long-term success in the media industry. Harper Fellowship Tracks and Learning Areas The Harper Fellowship program includes several fellowship tracks that allow participants to specialize in different areas of journalism and media operations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The fellowship tracks include: Reporting Audience engagement News product Development (new track) Reporting Track Fellows in the reporting track gain experience covering stories through in-depth reporting and investigative work. Participants work closely with editors and journalists to produce original reporting that focuses on the intersection of gender, politics, and policy. Reporting fellows are encouraged to develop their own story ideas, collaborate with newsroom teams, and experiment with storytelling across digital platforms. Audience Engagement Track The audience engagement track focuses on helping fellows develop skills in digital storytelling and audience-centered journalism. Participants learn how to distribute journalism across multiple platforms such as websites, social media, newsletters, and search engines. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); This track also involves creating engaging social media content and collaborating with editorial and creative teams to produce compelling headlines, visuals, and storytelling formats. News Product Track The Harper Fellowship news product track introduces fellows to product design and innovation in journalism. Participants learn how to develop features and digital tools that improve audience experience and support newsroom goals. Fellows work with product and technology teams to test ideas, analyze user feedback, and contribute to the development of journalism products such as newsletters, digital platforms, and audience engagement tools. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Development Track The newly introduced Harper Fellowship development track focuses on the business and fundraising side of nonprofit journalism. Fellows gain hands-on experience in donor engagement, membership campaigns, and fundraising strategy. Participants also learn how nonprofit news organizations sustain independent journalism through diversified revenue models and community support. Benefits of the Harper Fellowship The Harper Fellowship provides a comprehensive support system designed to help fellows build long-term careers in journalism. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Key benefits include: A full-time salaried fellowship with benefits One year of mentorship and professional development Career coaching and job placement support Networking opportunities with media professionals Membership in the National Association of Black Journalists Attendance at the NABJ convention and career fair Access to journalism training workshops and seminars A professional development stipend A work-from-home stipend and wellness stipend An Apple MacBook laptop provided for the fellowship year These resources ensure fellows receive both financial and professional support throughout the program. Harper Fellowship Professional Development A major component of the Harper Fellowship program is its strong emphasis on professional growth. Fellows participate in training workshops and development sessions covering topics such as: Networking and career advancement Salary negotiation Resume and portfolio building Mental health and burnout prevention Financial literacy Media industry insights from experienced journalists The program also includes career coaching and mentorship to help fellows transition successfully into long-term journalism careers after completing the fellowship. Harper Fellowship Retreats and Networking The Harper Fellowship experience includes several retreats and networking events designed to connect fellows with media professionals and industry leaders. The fellowship year begins with an opening retreat that introduces new fellows to the program and provides opportunities to connect with previous fellowship cohorts. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Midway through the fellowship, participants attend a career development retreat focused on networking and preparing for future opportunities in journalism. Fellows also attend an annual staff retreat with The 19th team, allowing them to engage in strategic discussions, professional learning sessions, and collaborative activities. These events help fellows build meaningful professional relationships and expand their journalism networks. How to Apply for the Harper Fellowship Applicants interested in the Harper Fellowship 2026 2027 must submit an application through The 19th s official application platform. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); The application typically requires: A cover letter explaining interest in the fellowship and career goals A resume outlining relevant experience Work samples demonstrating journalism or related skills Applicants should clearly describe how the Harper Fellowship aligns with their career aspirations and how they plan to contribute to public-service journalism. APPLY HERE Deadline Applications for the Harper Fellowship 2026 2027 must be submitted before the official deadline. Application Deadline: March 20, 2026 (5:00 p.m. PST) Applicants are encouraged to submit their materials early to avoid last-minute technical issues. Final Note The Harper Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for emerging journalists to gain hands-on newsroom experience while working alongside experienced media professionals. Through mentorship, professional training, and networking opportunities, the program equips fellows with the tools they need to build impactful careers in journalism. For individuals passionate about storytelling, public-service journalism, and media innovation, the Harper Fellowship can serve as a powerful launchpad for a long-term career in the industry. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); For more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and leadership programs, click here . title: World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 (Fully Funded) — Call for Applications in South Korea
summary: Overview Applications are now open for the World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 , a fully funded international programme organized within the framework of the UNESCO World Heritage Education Programme . Hosted by the Republic of Korea through the Korea Heritage Service (KHS) and the Korean National Commission for UNESCO (KNCU), the forum will take place from 13 21 July 2026 alongside the 48th Session of the World Heritage Committee . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Under the theme World Heritage, Communities and Education: Empowering Youth as Agents of Change, the programme will bring together 30 young professionals from around the world to strengthen their knowledge, skills, and engagement in the protection and management of natural and cultural World Heritage sites. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Forum Theme and Objectives The 2026 forum focuses on the relationship between World Heritage, communities, and education, with the goal of empowering youth to play an active role in heritage conservation and governance. Key objectives include: Strengthening the commitment and professional capacity of young practitioners working in heritage-related fields Exploring how community participation can improve conservation and management practices Examining the role of education, research, and learning in informed decision-making Promoting youth leadership and inclusive governance in World Heritage protection Through exchanges with local and international experts, participants will engage in collaborative learning and develop practical perspectives on sustainable heritage management. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Programme Structure The forum combines academic discussions with practical, site-based learning experiences at World Heritage properties in the Republic of Korea. Activities include: Thematic presentations and expert-led discussions Collaborative workshops and group work Site visits and field-based learning Peer exchange with international participants Joint session with the World Heritage Site Managers Forum At the conclusion of the programme, participants will collectively prepare and present a Youth Declaration to the 48th Session of the World Heritage Committee. Provisional Schedule 12 July 2026: Arrival of participants 13 July 2026: Opening of the forum and introductions 14 19 July 2026: Thematic sessions, workshops, and site-based learning 18 July 2026: Joint session with the World Heritage Site Managers Forum 19 July 2026: Drafting of the Youth Declaration 20 July 2026: Finalisation and presentation of the Youth Declaration 21 July 2026: Closing session and departure Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Age Between 23 and 32 years old (born between 1994 and 2003 inclusive) Professional Profile Applicants should be young professionals or practitioners engaged in one or more of the following areas: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Education and teaching in formal learning settings World Heritage site management Research and innovation related to World Heritage NGOs or community-level associations focused on heritage Additional Requirements Demonstrated involvement in World Heritage-related activities Strong motivation to contribute to discussions on youth, communities, and education Full availability for the entire duration of the forum Proficiency in English (the working language of the programme) Selection Considerations The selection process aims to ensure: Balanced geographical representation Gender equality Diversity of professional backgrounds Potential for knowledge-sharing and impact after the forum Funding and Benefits The World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 is fully funded. Selected participants will receive: International economy-class travel Accommodation Local transportation Meals during the programme Visa support and related fees where applicable Participants wishing to extend their stay after the programme must cover additional expenses independently. Application Process Interested candidates must apply through the official online application form: Application Link: https://unesco-2023.limesurvey.net/662537 Application Deadline: 15 March 2026 (23:59 CET) Important notes: Only complete applications submitted through the online form will be considered Selection is based solely on the information provided in the application Recommendations or endorsements are not required Selected applicants will be notified via email. Contact Information For questions regarding the application process: Email: For any questions, kindly contact: wh-ypf@unesco.org Why You Should Apply The World Heritage Young Professionals Forum is a flagship initiative of UNESCO s World Heritage Education Programme. It offers young professionals a unique opportunity to: Build international networks with peers and experts Gain practical experience through site-based learning Deepen understanding of the World Heritage Convention Contribute directly to global discussions on heritage conservation Strengthen leadership skills in community-based and sustainable heritage management For professionals working at the intersection of heritage, education, and community engagement, the forum provides both international exposure and long-term professional value. Call for Applications Selection of participants will be carried out solely on the basis of the online applications, in accordance with the criteria specified in the Call for Applications. Recommendations, nominations, or endorsements will therefore not be considered. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Online application Applications must be submitted through the online application form by 15 March 2026 at 23:59 CET Application Link: https://unesco-2023.limesurvey.net/662537 For any questions, kindly contact: wh-ypf@unesco.org For more info please visit: https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/2862/ The post World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 (Fully Funded) — Call for Applications in South Korea appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Headstream Accelerator 2026 : $30,000 Non-Dilutive Funding for Digital Youth Mental Health Startups in the U.S.
summary: Transforming Teen Mental Well-Being Through Technology and Youth Co-Creation The Headstream Accelerator 2026 (Cohort VI) is a four-month, virtual-first startup accelerator designed to help early-stage entrepreneurs scale digital solutions that improve the mental well-being of teens at risk. Focused on the education and healthcare sectors, the program provides funding, coaching, expert mentorship, and direct youth input to ensure solutions are culturally relevant, age-appropriate, and impactful. Headstream supports founders from all backgrounds who are building tech-enabled products that address critical youth mental health challenges across the United States. The accelerator is particularly committed to supporting innovations that reach historically excluded communities and embed youth voices throughout product development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); If you are developing a digital solution aimed at youth mental well-being and are ready to refine your business model, strengthen your growth strategy, and expand your impact, this program offers both financial support and structured guidance to help you scale responsibly and sustainably. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Program Overview: What Makes the Headstream Accelerator Unique The Headstream Accelerator is not just about funding it is about building resilient, impact-driven companies that can sustainably serve youth mental health needs. The four-month program includes: Virtual cohort-based learning experiences Access to expert advisors and investors Youth co-creation sessions Personalized one-on-one and group coaching A required in-person kickoff event Structured growth planning and scaling support The accelerator runs from July through November 2026 , beginning with a virtual program start in early July and concluding in early November. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); $30,000 Non-Dilutive Stipend and Additional Benefits Selected participants will receive: $30,000 Non-Dilutive Stipend This funding does not require equity, allowing founders to grow their ventures without giving up ownership. The stipend is provided at the end of the 16-week program. Youth Co-Creation Partnership Participants collaborate directly with youth from Headstream s Youth Co-Creator Club. This ensures that products: Reflect cultural relevance Address real youth needs Are age-appropriate Provide an engaging user experience This youth-centered approach strengthens product-market fit and long-term sustainability. Access to Advisors and Investors Founders gain access to: A network of expert advisors A community of fellow founders Potential investors and capital providers Coaching with FounderWell Participants receive one-on-one and group coaching sessions through Headstream s partner, FounderWell, to refine leadership skills, operational strategy, and business sustainability. Participant Expectations Entrepreneurs selected for Cohort VI are expected to: Attend all required virtual cohort sessions Participate in youth co-creation workshops Join advisor calls and office hours Attend the in-person kickoff event from July 28 30, 2026 Commit approximately 2 5 hours per week during the program Embed youth voices into product development This structure ensures accountability, engagement, and measurable progress. Who Should Apply? The Headstream Accelerator supports early-stage digital solutions focused on youth mental well-being, particularly those serving historically excluded communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); You are encouraged to apply if you: Have a working prototype Are preparing for launch Are ready to scale Operate within or adjacent to education or healthcare Are building a tech-enabled solution Important: Idea-stage submissions without a functional prototype are not eligible. Eligible applicant types include: Startups Social enterprises Research institutions Non-profits For-profit ventures While non-profits are eligible, all applicants must demonstrate a sustainable funding model. Selection Criteria Applicants must demonstrate: Clear impact on youth mental well-being A focus on U.S.-based youth, particularly ages 13 19 (pre-teen and college-aged solutions are also eligible) A functional prototype A sustainable funding model A clear growth strategy Commitment to inclusivity U.S. market focus (international applicants must have a U.S. presence) Full Timeline for Headstream Accelerator 2026 Applications Open: January 15, 2026 Application Deadline: March 15, 2026 Interviews: May 22 June 4, 2026 Cohort Notification: Week of June 15, 2026 Virtual Program Start: Week of July 6, 2026 In-Person Kickoff: July 28 30, 2026 Program End: Week of November 2, 2026 Applicants may edit submissions until the application deadline. All submissions become final once the deadline passes. Virtual Info Sessions Interested founders can join a virtual information session: January 21 at 1 pm PST February 11 at 1 pm PST March 11 at 1 pm PST Registration links are available through the official Headstream Accelerator webpage. Frequently Asked Questions Do I need to generate revenue? No, but you must have a functional prototype, a sustainable funding model, and a growth plan. Are non-profits eligible? Yes, provided they demonstrate financial sustainability. Can solo founders apply? Yes. Having collaborators or contractors is encouraged but not required. When is the stipend provided? At the end of the 16-week program. What happens after applying? Applicants will be notified during the week of June 15, 2026. Selected candidates will be invited for interviews. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Why This Accelerator Matters in 2026 Youth mental health continues to be one of the most urgent public health challenges in the United States. Digital solutions have the power to scale support, increase accessibility, and reduce stigma especially for teens in historically underserved communities. The Headstream Accelerator provides funding, mentorship, and youth-driven validation to ensure that emerging innovations are both effective and sustainable. If you are building a digital solution for teen mental well-being and are ready to scale your impact in the U.S. market, this accelerator offers a structured pathway to growth. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Application Deadline: March 15, 2026 For more information click here Apply here Discover more opportunities here The post Headstream Accelerator 2026 : $30,000 Non-Dilutive Funding for Digital Youth Mental Health Startups in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Canary Impact 2026 Leadership Opportunities: $10,000 Awards for Justice-Involved Leaders Through the 10-for-10 Program and NextWave Leadership Program (U.S.)
summary: Canary Impact is advancing justice reform across the United States through two transformative leadership initiatives: the 10-for-10 Program and the NextWave Leadership Program. Both programs are designed to invest directly in justice-involved leaders who are committed to dismantling systemic inequities in the criminal legal system. Through catalytic $10,000 financial awards, mentorship, structured leadership development, and access to a powerful national network, Canary Impact supports individuals with lived experience of incarceration to transform their ideas into systemic change. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); If you are a justice-involved leader in the United States with an innovative idea for reform, these programs provide a platform, community, and financial support to accelerate your impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); 1. The 10-for-10 Program: Catalyzing Systemic Change Through Lived Experience Overview of the 10-for-10 Program The 10-for-10 Program is Canary Impact s flagship initiative designed to uplift justice-involved leaders of all ages. The program is rooted in the belief that those directly impacted by the criminal legal system possess critical expertise and vision needed to dismantle systemic inequities nationwide. By providing financial awards, mentorship, strategic support, and a collaborative peer network, the 10-for-10 Program creates pathways for participants to channel lived experience into actionable solutions that transform communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The program focuses on long-term systemic change rather than short-term interventions. It is structured as a leadership journey that builds confidence, expands networks, and strengthens reform strategies. What Sets 10-for-10 Apart? Betting on Success Canary Impact invests directly in people with lived experience, recognizing that their leadership is essential to building equitable systems. A National Platform Participants become part of a nationwide community of justice reform visionaries working on issues such as: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); LGBTQIA+ advocacy Disability rights Reentry initiatives Sentencing reform Decarceration efforts Community-based justice solutions Driving Long-Term Impact By supporting experimentation and bold ideas, the program fosters ripple effects that extend throughout the United States. Program Benefits $10,000 Catalytic Investment Each selected participant receives a $10,000 financial award to advance their leadership journey and implement transformative initiatives. Collaborative Network Participants join a national community of justice-involved leaders committed to reform and shared learning. Cohort-Based Learning A supportive peer network encourages collaboration, collective action, and knowledge exchange. Tailored Leadership Support Participants receive mentorship and strategic guidance from Canary Impact s nationally recognized coaches and alumni. Eligibility Criteria for 10-for-10 Applicants must: Have a history of incarceration Reside in the United States Focus their work within U.S. communities Have an innovative idea that leads to systemic change Propose work that does not involve lobbying Not have previously received the 10-for-10 Prize Click HERE to learn more and apply for the 10-for-10 Program. 2. NextWave Leadership Program: Empowering Justice-Involved Youth (Ages 18 25) Overview of the NextWave Leadership Program The NextWave Leadership Program is Canary Impact s youth-focused initiative designed for justice-involved leaders aged 18 25. Recognizing that young people are disproportionately impacted by the criminal legal system, NextWave provides tailored mentorship, financial investment, leadership development, and community support to empower youth as change-makers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); NextWave is structured as a launchpad for emerging leaders to challenge systemic inequities and build long-term advocacy careers. What Makes NextWave Unique? Targeted Youth Support The program addresses challenges specific to young justice-involved individuals, including: Reentry barriers Generational trauma Structural inequities Investing in People, Not Just Projects The program prioritizes personal leadership development alongside project implementation. Catalyzing Systemic Change By supporting youth leaders, NextWave strengthens the movement toward decarceration and justice reform. Program Highlights $10,000 Financial Investment Each participant receives a $10,000 award to support their leadership development, education, advocacy, or community initiatives. Comprehensive Leadership Development Participants engage in structured curriculum covering: Advocacy Justice reform Leadership strategy Communication skills Resilience and systems change Cohort Community Participants join a peer network fostering collaboration and collective action. Capacity Building for Long-Term Success Through mentorship and skill development, participants build sustainable leadership pathways. Eligibility Criteria for NextWave Applicants must: Be 18 25 years old Have a history of incarceration Reside in the United States Focus work within U.S. communities Propose work that does not involve lobbying Not have previously received the 10-for-10 Prize Click HERE to learn more and apply for the NextWave Leadership Program. Program Philosophy: Investing in Lived Expertise Both programs operate on a shared belief: People who have experienced incarceration firsthand are uniquely positioned to lead transformative justice reform. By investing in leaders rather than institutions, Canary Impact builds a movement rooted in lived experience, community accountability, and systemic change. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Application Process Interested candidates should apply through the official Canary Impact website under either: 10-for-10 Program NextWave Leadership Program Applicants are encouraged to present a clear, innovative idea for systemic change and demonstrate readiness to engage in cohort-based leadership development. Application deadlines are not specified on the site. Applicants should consult the official Canary Impact website for current timelines. Discover more Awards on OFY HERE . title: George C. Marshall Fellowship 2026: Develop Grand Strategy Skills and Advance U.S. National Security Leadership
summary: Introduction: A Premier Fellowship in Grand Strategy and Strategic Leadership The George C. Marshall Fellowship is a prestigious leadership development program offered by The Heritage Foundation . Designed for exceptional conservative professionals early in their careers, this fellowship provides a comprehensive foundation in grand strategy theory and practice , strategic leadership, and the integration of national power instruments to advance U.S. national security interests. Inspired by the legacy of George C. Marshall , the program equips fellows with the intellectual framework and practical tools needed to address complex global challenges while preserving the principles of free enterprise, limited government, individual freedom, traditional American values, and a strong national defense. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The 2025 George C. Marshall Fellowship is structured as an immersive, multi-month experience combining expert-led discussions, hands-on workshops, historical analysis, and real-world strategic exercises. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the George C. Marshall Fellowship Mission The mission of the George C. Marshall Fellows program is to provide exceptional conservative professionals with the opportunity to: Apply the principles of grand strategy. Integrate all instruments of national power (diplomatic, informational, military, and economic). Develop practical approaches to achieving U.S. national security objectives. Strengthen leadership capabilities grounded in conservative policy principles. Vision The program offers a comprehensive overview of grand strategy theory and its practical application to today s global challenges. It is designed for individuals early in their careers who are likely to take on significant responsibilities in advancing U.S. national security. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants gain: Exposure to leading national security experts. Practical insights into defense and foreign policy. A structured understanding of geopolitical threats. Real-world exercises simulating strategic decision-making. The fellowship emphasizes both theoretical depth and applied leadership training , ensuring participants leave with actionable knowledge. 2026 George C. Marshall Fellowship Program Schedule The 2026 cohort follows a structured curriculum running from April through September. Program Launch April 30 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. This opening session introduces fellows to the program s intellectual framework and sets expectations for the months ahead. Defense Budget 101 May 14 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Practical briefing and workshop on the Defense Budget. This session provides an in-depth understanding of: The U.S. defense budgeting process. Strategic resource allocation. Budget priorities and trade-offs. How funding decisions reflect national strategy. Participants engage in hands-on analysis to connect policy objectives with fiscal realities. Gettysburg Staff Ride May 29 (All Day) Day trip, lunch, and battlefield tour in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania. This immersive historical experience at Gettysburg Battlefield allows fellows to: Study strategic decision-making in historical context. Analyze leadership under pressure. Evaluate operational successes and failures. Draw modern lessons from Civil War strategy. Staff rides are a traditional military leadership development tool, reinforcing critical thinking and situational analysis. Module 2: Deterring China June 11 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. June 25 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Practical exercise. This module focuses on: The strategic challenge posed by China. Deterrence theory and application. Military, economic, and diplomatic tools. Scenario-based strategic exercises. Fellows examine current geopolitical tensions and develop policy recommendations within a structured framework. Module 3: Hemispheric Security July 9 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. July 23 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and panel discussion. This module addresses: Western Hemisphere security dynamics. U.S. relations with regional partners. Emerging threats in the Americas. Strategic coordination across institutions. Participants engage with policy experts and panelists to explore multidimensional security challenges. Program Graduation September 10 (Time and location TBD) Dinner and happy hour. The program concludes with a formal graduation event recognizing fellows achievements and fostering long-term professional networks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why Apply for the George C. Marshall Fellowship? The fellowship provides: Advanced training in grand strategy. Direct engagement with national security experts. Applied policy workshops. Leadership development grounded in conservative principles. Networking with peers committed to advancing U.S. national security. Exposure to real-world policy analysis and strategic planning. A prestigious credential associated with The Heritage Foundation. This is not merely a lecture series. It is an immersive strategic leadership development experience. Application Process Interested candidates are encouraged to apply through the official application portal.
